title: pouteria sandwicensis f. puulupensis
intro: Pouteria sandwicensis f. puulupensis is a variety of tree belonging to the family Sapotaceae, commonly found in Hawaii. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its bark, which contains various compounds with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. Pouteria sandwicensis f. puulupensis is used for its health benefits, including wound healing, fever reduction, and relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In traditional Hawaiian medicine, the bark is often prepared as a decoction or infusion for herbal remedies, and it is also used as a poultice for skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis.
